laws.title: Indiana Minimum Wage — $7.25/hr (Federal Rate)
laws.summary: Indiana does not set a state minimum wage above the federal rate. All covered employers must pay non-exempt employees at least $7.25 per hour under the federal Fair Labor Standards Act. Tipped employees may be paid $2.13/hour by the employer provided tips bring total compensation to at least $7.25/hour.
compliance_actions: Pay all non-exempt employees at least $7.25/hour
For tipped employees, ensure the employer-paid wage plus tips equals at least $7.25/hour
Display the Indiana Minimum Wage poster in a visible workplace location
Retain payroll records showing hours worked and wages paid for at least 3 years
